title: mentha aquatica var. macrophylla
intro: Mentha aquatica var. macrophylla, commonly known as large-leaved water mint, is a variant of the water mint plant, characterized by its large, lance-shaped leaves. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to act as a carminative, antispasmodic, and anti-inflammatory agent, making it useful in treating digestive issues and alleviating menstrual cramps. The health benefits of large-leaved water mint include its ability to aid in digestion, relieve nausea and headaches, and help to reduce stress and anxiety. Large-leaved water mint can be prepared as herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being.
